description: DogStatsD-to-Forwarder Pipeline Memory Optimisations - Reduce allocs/op across the full metric pipeline (parsing → aggregation → serialisation → forwarding) by ≥25% via sync.Pool recycling and pre-sized data structures

userStories.title: Establish CPU and Memory Allocation Baseline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reproducible profiling baseline for the full pipeline so that I can measure the impact of every subsequent optimisation against real data.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `go test -bench=. -benchmem -count=5 ./comp/dogstatsd/server/... ./pkg/aggregator/... ./pkg/serializer/... ./comp/forwarder/defaultforwarder/...` and save output to `plans/bench-baseline-forwarder.txt`
Run `go test -bench=. -memprofile=mem.out -cpuprofile=cpu.out -benchmem ./pkg/aggregator/...` to generate pprof profiles
A `scripts/profile_pipeline.sh` shell script is created that documents the exact commands to reproduce the baseline profiles
Top 10 allocation sites by cumulative allocs/op are documented in `plans/profiling-baseline.md`
Top 10 CPU hotspots are documented in `plans/profiling-baseline.md`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pool MetricSample Structs Across the Parsing→Aggregator Boundary
userStories.description: As a developer, I want MetricSample structs to be pooled and reused rather than heap-allocated per metric so that GC pressure in the high-frequency parsing path is reduced.
acceptanceCriteria: A `sync.Pool` for `MetricSample` structs is introduced in `pkg/metrics` or `comp/dogstatsd/server`
`MetricSample` structs are obtained from the pool in the parsing worker (`comp/dogstatsd/server/server_worker.go`) instead of being freshly allocated
Structs are returned to the pool after the TimeSampler has consumed them (after `sample()` completes in `pkg/aggregator/time_sampler.go`)
Tags (`[]string`) field is copied or fully interned before the struct is returned to the pool — no dangling references
`BenchmarkTimeSamplerSampleTagCount` allocs/op is ≥20% lower than `plans/bench-baseline-forwarder.txt`
`go test -race ./comp/dogstatsd/server/... ./pkg/aggregator/...` passes with no data race
All unit tests pass: `go test ./comp/dogstatsd/server/... ./pkg/aggregator/...`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Reduce Tag Slice Allocations in Context Key Generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tag slice handling in contextResolver.trackContext() to allocate fewer intermediate slices so that context key generation is cheaper per sample.
acceptanceCriteria: `HashingTagsAccumulator` in `pkg/aggregator/context_resolver.go` reuses a pre-allocated backing buffer per TimeSampler worker goroutine (using `sync.Pool` or per-worker struct field), avoiding a fresh `[]string` allocation per `trackContext()` call
The sort in key generation operates on the in-place buffer rather than a copy where the API permits
`BenchmarkContextResolver1000` allocs/op is ≥25% lower than `plans/bench-baseline-forwarder.txt`
`BenchmarkTimeSamplerSampleHighCardinality` (100K contexts) ns/op shows no regression vs baseline
All unit tests pass: `go test ./pkg/aggregator/...`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pool Serie Structs and Point Slices in TimeSampler Flush
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Serie structs and their []Point backing arrays to be pooled across flush cycles so that the flush path allocates less per cycle.
acceptanceCriteria: A `SeriePool` using `sync.Pool` is introduced in `pkg/metrics` or `pkg/aggregator` to recycle `Serie` structs
Point slices within pooled `Serie` structs are pre-allocated to the expected capacity (number of flush buckets in the Serie's window) to avoid append-driven growth allocations
Pooled `Serie` structs are fully zeroed/reset before reuse to prevent stale field leakage between flush cycles
`BenchmarkTimeSamplerFlushCardinality` (10K contexts) allocs/op is ≥30% lower than `plans/bench-baseline-forwarder.txt`
`BenchmarkTimeSamplerFlushMetricTypes` shows no regression in ns/op vs baseline
All unit tests pass: `go test ./pkg/aggregator/... ./pkg/metrics/...`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Reduce Allocation in SketchMap Insertion and Flush
userStories.description: As a developer, I want sketch (distribution metric) insertion and flush to avoid creating unnecessary intermediate map entries and slice copies so that distribution metrics have lower allocation cost.
acceptanceCriteria: Inner map initialisation in `sketchMap` (`pkg/aggregator/sketch_map.go`) uses `make(map[...], hint)` with a hint derived from the previous flush's cardinality (tracked as a rolling average) to reduce rehashing allocations
`SketchPoint` slices in `flushBefore()` are pre-allocated to the expected capacity (number of timestamp buckets) rather than growing via append
`BenchmarkTimeSamplerFlushMetricTypes` for distribution type allocs/op is ≥20% lower than `plans/bench-baseline-forwarder.txt`
Existing sketch quantile accuracy tests pass: `go test ./pkg/util/quantile/...`
All unit tests pass: `go test ./pkg/aggregator/...`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Reduce Buffer Allocations in Serialisation Streaming Path
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the IterableSeries and IterableSketches streaming paths to reuse encode buffers between payload chunks so that serialisation allocates fewer intermediate byte slices per flush.
acceptanceCriteria: Encoding buffers (`bytes.Buffer` or `[]byte`) in the serialiser (`pkg/serializer/serializer.go` and `pkg/serializer/internal/metrics/`) are obtained from a `sync.Pool` rather than freshly allocated per payload chunk
Pooled buffers are returned to the pool only after the encoded payload has been handed off to the forwarder (not while a channel consumer may still hold a reference)
`json_payload_builder_throughput_benchmark_test.go` allocs/op is ≥20% lower than `plans/bench-baseline-forwarder.txt`
Payload content is byte-identical to pre-optimisation output (validated by existing serialiser correctness tests: `go test ./pkg/serializer/...`)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pool Forwarder Transaction Objects
userStories.description: As a developer, I want HTTP transaction objects in the forwarder to be pooled and reused so that high-frequency metric submissions allocate fewer objects on the heap.
acceptanceCriteria: `transaction.HTTPTransaction` structs are obtained from a `sync.Pool` in `comp/forwarder/defaultforwarder` rather than being freshly allocated per `SubmitV1Series()` / `SubmitSketch()` call
Transaction structs are fully reset before reuse: headers cleared, payload reference nilled, retry state zeroed
Transactions are returned to the pool only after the HTTP response is fully processed (success or terminal 4xx failure) — not while the retry queue still holds a reference
Retry logic in `domain_forwarder.go` correctly handles pooled transactions with no double-return to pool on retried transactions
`forwarder_bench_test.go` allocs/op is ≥15% lower than `plans/bench-baseline-forwarder.txt`
`go test -race ./comp/forwarder/...` passes with no data race
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Validate Cumulative Allocation Reduction Against Baseline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a final benchmark comparison between the optimised pipeline and the baseline profiles to confirm that cumulative allocs/op reduction meets targets with no regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `go test -bench=. -benchmem -count=5 ./comp/dogstatsd/server/... ./pkg/aggregator/... ./pkg/serializer/... ./comp/forwarder/defaultforwarder/...` and save output to `plans/bench-final-forwarder.txt`
Total allocs/op across all pipeline benchmarks in `plans/bench-final-forwarder.txt` is ≥25% lower than `plans/bench-baseline-forwarder.txt` (verified with `benchstat` comparison)
`BenchmarkTimeSamplerSampleThenFlushCycle` ns/op is not worse than baseline (within 5%)
All existing benchmark tests show no regression >5% in ns/op vs baseline
A human-readable comparison table (benchstat output) is appended to `plans/bench-final-forwarder.txt`
Updated profiling summary is appended to `plans/profiling-baseline.md` alongside the original baseline for side-by-side comparison
`go test -race ./comp/dogstatsd/... ./pkg/aggregator/... ./pkg/serializer/... ./comp/forwarder/...` passes
Typecheck passes
